title: why WILLE was never able to beat NERV or the opposite during the 14 years of the timeskip
display_title: why WILLE was never able to beat NERV or the opposite during the 14 years of the timeskip
wikitext:
[]: why WILLE was never able to beat NERV or the opposite, during the 14 years of the timeskip?             Quote Post by The18°angel » Fri May 21, 2021 11:41 am put your theories for why WILLE could never defeat NERV behind plot convenience during the timeskip.

[]: Re: why WILLE was never able to beat NERV during the 14 years of the timeskip?             Quote Post by Konja7 » Fri May 21, 2021 11:58 am In fact, Neo-Nerv has a lot more "Eva power" than WILLE. I mean WILLE only has two Eva (and can't build anymore), while Neo-Nerv can build series of Eva. WILLE also has to supoort the Villages. tweet-body — twitter-user (@username) date document.querySelectorAll('blockquote.twitter-tweet > a').forEach((t) => t.href = t.href.replace('x.com', 'twitter.com')) Yamashita has mentioned that neo-NERV's material feels like mass-produced tools made in automated factories, while WILLE's vibe is "hastily constructed collection of junk" PS: The question should be why Neo-Nerv hasn't beat WILLE in the timeskip.

[]: Re: why WILLE was never able to beat NERV during the 14 years of the timeskip?             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Fri May 21, 2021 3:56 pm Konja7 wrote: PS: The question should be why Neo-Nerv hasn't beat WILLE in the timeskip.

[]: Re: why WILLE was never able to beat NERV or the opposite, during the 14 years of the timeskip?             Quote Post by swagbuckking1 » Fri May 21, 2021 8:16 pm Just a funny thought I had. Imagine if as soon as Shinji woke up, Misato asked him to pilot Unit 1 again and use the awakened powers to nuke the geofront and NERV's headquarters. Just imagine how much easier WILLE's lives would become (assuming Shinji did it no questions asked). Theoretically NERV would be defenseless against an awakened Unit 1; there's only so much Mark 09 could do, Unit 13 wasn't finished at that point and the nemesis series have proven to be no match for Unit 1. It's pretty funny to imagine the shock on Gendo's face as Shinji comes descending down with his eye lasers and AT field projectiles.

[]: Re: why WILLE was never able to beat NERV or the opposite, during the 14 years of the timeskip?             Quote Post by Konja7 » Fri May 21, 2021 8:39 pm swagbuckking1 wrote: Just a funny thought I had. Imagine if as soon as Shinji woke up, Misato asked him to pilot Unit 1 again and use the awakened powers to nuke the geofront and NERV's headquarters. Just imagine how much easier WILLE's lives would become (assuming Shinji did it no questions asked). Theoretically NERV would be defenseless against an awakened Unit 1; there's only so much Mark 09 could do, Unit 13 wasn't finished at that point and the nemesis series have proven to be no match for Unit 1. It's pretty funny to imagine the shock on Gendo's face as Shinji comes descending down with his eye lasers and AT field projectiles.

[]: Re: why WILLE was never able to beat NERV or the opposite, during the 14 years of the timeskip?             Quote Post by swagbuckking1 » Fri May 21, 2021 10:05 pm Well my post wasn't meant to be taken seriously and it was under the liberal assumption that Shinji would do it not knowing his father was there. Also wasn't it Shinji entering Unit 1 that allowed him to talk to Rei to reveal his true synch scores? After all, his synch scores were actually ∞%, but were disguised by Rei to look like they were 0%; he still could have synched with Unit 1 at the beginning of Q if he wanted to.

[]: Re: why WILLE was never able to beat NERV or the opposite, during the 14 years of the timeskip?             Quote Post by Konja7 » Fri May 21, 2021 10:21 pm swagbuckking1 wrote: Well my post wasn't meant to be taken seriously and it was under the liberal assumption that Shinji would do it not knowing his father was there. Also wasn't it Shinji entering Unit 1 that allowed him to talk to Rei to reveal his true synch scores? After all, his synch scores were actually ∞%, but were disguised by Rei to look like they were 0%; he still could have synched with Unit 1 at the beginning of Q if he wanted to.

[]: Re: why WILLE was never able to beat NERV during the 14 years of the timeskip?             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sat May 22, 2021 4:00 am ElMariachi wrote: Konja7#923350 wrote: PS: The question should be why Neo-Nerv hasn't beat WILLE in the timeskip.

[]: Re: why WILLE was never able to beat NERV during the 14 years of the timeskip?             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sat May 22, 2021 5:08 am Kendrix wrote: Probably this - At least Gendo probably didn't think they're too big a threat/ it's not too bad if they live cause they can be useful. Other than that, Neo NERV is forced to rely on automatic tools that might not listen to too complex instructions (hence why ReiQ was sent to get Shinji and not a random nemesis series) and Misato is pretty resourceful & tenacious. The last 14 years were probably WILLE swatting at, running from & using guerilla tactics against overwhelming enemy forces

[]: Re: why WILLE was never able to beat NERV during the 14 years of the timeskip?             Quote Post by Konja7 » Sat May 22, 2021 5:46 am ElMariachi wrote: That's why I said that the post-N3I world building in the light of Shin doesn't make any sense, because if you stop thinking about it for five minutes it completely crumbles: the power balance between WILLE and neo-NERV/SEELE is so hilariously one-sided that there's no reason for the bad guys to not have crushed them and finished their plans unhindered, except that the plot required it. Hell, the bad guys even get the infinite resources cheat code that let them continuously build new Evas (we see this factory in Q and it's still churning out Eva body parts)

[]: Re: why WILLE was never able to beat NERV during the 14 years of the timeskip?             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sat May 22, 2021 6:53 pm Konja7 wrote: One theory I have is that Kaworu agreed to work with Neo-NERV in exchange for them not atracking/crushing WILLE and the Villages. Kaworu seems to have a lot of appreciation for Kaji and maybe Misato. Also, the DSS choker (used by all WILLE pilots) was created for him. Of course, this wouldn't make WILLE to look more competent.

[]: Re: why WILLE was never able to beat NERV or the opposite, during the 14 years of the timeskip?             Quote Post by wiser3754 » Sun May 23, 2021 3:53 pm About time this topic was brought up! I was always skeptical that WILLE never had a chance as they were outnumbered, outgunned, outclassed and more importantly, out of time! Also Fuyutsuki is a master of Shogi so he always plans and predicts at least two steps ahead. That "out of time" part was my specualtion that Gendo's visor was a chronovisor which allowed him to see forward in time giving Nerv a very big advantage.

[]: Re: why WILLE was never able to beat NERV or the opposite, during the 14 years of the timeskip?             Quote Post by Blockio » Sun May 23, 2021 5:40 pm Being good at a board game != being good at real world strategy. I won't say that it's impossible that Nerv simply played with Wille, but as it currently stands, we don't know enough about the logistics of both sides to really say for certain. Nerv wasn't in a great position either, and the fact that they didn't use their fancy human-type Mark.04s implies that they had a reason not to, which also coincides with them sending just Mark.09 to abduct Shinj instead of providing support from other units; to the extent of information currently available, there's a good chance that up until Shin, Nerv simply didn't have the operational capacities to take out Wille for good

[]: Re: why WILLE was never able to beat NERV or the opposite, during the 14 years of the timeskip?             Quote Post by Konja7 » Sun May 23, 2021 5:56 pm Blockio wrote: to the extent of information currently available, there's a good chance that up until Shin, Nerv simply didn't have the operational capacities to take out Wille for good

[]: Re: why WILLE was never able to beat NERV or the opposite, during the 14 years of the timeskip?             Quote Post by Blockio » Sun May 23, 2021 7:00 pm There's another difference: They are no longer occupied with building Eva 13. The specifics of the logistic side of things are mushy as ever, but I'm gonna go out on a limb and say that constructing an artificial god is not the most economic project, and chances are most other production facilities are going to run on the back burner as a direct result thereof.

[]: Re: why WILLE was never able to beat NERV during the 14 years of the timeskip?             Quote Post by DantesInferno » Sun May 23, 2021 11:05 pm ElMariachi wrote: [...]A way to bring the second Impact Trigger they needed to breach the seal to Lilith's Chamber

[]: Re: why WILLE was never able to beat NERV or the opposite, during the 14 years of the timeskip?             Quote Post by swagbuckking1 » Mon May 24, 2021 6:26 am DantesInferno wrote: ElMariachi wrote: [...]A way to bring the second Impact Trigger they needed to breach the seal to Lilith's Chamber

[]: Re: why WILLE was never able to beat NERV during the 14 years of the timeskip?             Quote Post by Konja7 » Mon May 24, 2021 6:50 am DantesInferno wrote: What did I miss here? We know Shinji is the trigger for N3I, and Kaworu, that of N4I; But when did we figure out so much about second impact, so as to know who was the "trigger"? You mean Eva-13, which is assumed to be one of the original Adams?

[]: Re: why WILLE was never able to beat NERV during the 14 years of the timeskip?             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Mon May 24, 2021 3:20 pm DantesInferno wrote: What did I miss here? We know Shinji is the trigger for N3I, and Kaworu, that of N4I; But when did we figure out so much about second impact, so as to know who was the "trigger"? You mean Eva-13, which is assumed to be one of the original Adams?

[]: Re: why WILLE was never able to beat NERV during the 14 years of the timeskip?             Quote Post by DantesInferno » Mon May 24, 2021 5:32 pm ElMariachi wrote: swagbuckking1 and Konja7 had the right idea: I was talking about the fact that SEELE needed two pilots synchronizing to break through the seal to Lilith's Chamber, and that both EVA-13's pilots needed to be Impact Triggers (as in, able to awake an Eva and start an Impact) to serve as backup if something happens to one of the pilots. (that's why Fourth Impact didn't stopped until Mari ejected Shinji and Fuyutsuki noted that SEELE almost won)
